title: elatostema parvum var. parvum
intro: Elatostema parvum var. parvum, also known as Small River Elm or Chinese Water Clover, is a perennial herb native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and delicate stems. The medicinal properties of Elatostema parvum var. parvum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, as well as its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. Elatostema parvum var. parvum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of ailments.
